title: As Trump's immigration crackdown turns deadly, FBI moves ICE agent cases to HSI: Report
content:
• The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) will no longer investigate alleged assaults on immigration agents, deferring these cases to Homeland Security Investigations (HSI), a policy change reported on 19 July and communicated to FBI field officers on 16 July.
• On 7 July, an ICE officer fatally shot 52-year-old Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston, Texas, and six days later, 26-year-old Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero was killed in Biddeford, Maine, making them the 29th and 30th people shot by immigration officers since Trump took office in January last year.
• At least 22 people have died in ICE custody this year, including those in immigration detention centres.

title: Lamine Yamal Net Worth: Valuation to skyrocket after World Cup win as teenager shines in Spain vs Argentina final
content:
• Barcelona has placed a colossal $1.1 billion (₹10,591 crore) release clause on Lamine Yamal, securing him under a long-term contract until 2031.
• Lamine Yamal earns an annual base salary from Barcelona estimated between $18 million (₹173 crore) and $21.5 million (₹207 crore), with potential total earnings reaching $33 million (₹317 crore) including bonuses.
• In 2024, Lamine Yamal signed a landmark 10-year partnership with Adidas, reportedly valued at $34 million (₹327 crore).

title: ‘Shame on Argentina’: Social media erupts as chaos follows World Cup final; embarrassing history haunts Messi’s team
content:
• Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.
• Enzo Fernandez received a red card, marking him as the third Argentine and the sixth player overall to be sent off in a World Cup final.
• Enzo Fernandez and Paredes became the 6th and 7th players to receive red cards in a World Cup final, a double-sending-off for Argentina that repeated history 36 years after a similar incident in 1990 against West Germany.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Halftime Show: What happened during FIFA’s first-ever halftime spectacle?
content:
• The 2026 FIFA World Cup final, held on Sunday, 19 July, at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, introduced the tournament's first-ever official halftime show.
• The inaugural FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show featured Madonna, Shakira, and BTS as co-headliners, and was curated by Coldplay frontman Chris Martin.
• Proceeds from the show were set to support the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, which aims to raise $100 million for children's education and football opportunities globally.

title: Watch: Tom Cruise showcases football skills in Digger promo ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremony
content:
• The FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremony is scheduled for Sunday, July 19, 2026.
• The closing ceremony of the FIFA World Cup 2026 will take place at New York New Jersey Stadium.
• Actor Tom Cruise will make a special appearance at the FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremony to promote his film 'Digger,' which is set for release in October 2026.

title: Watching the FIFA World Cup final on July 20? Maharashtra lets restaurants stay open till 4 am
content:
• The Maharashtra government has allowed licensed restaurants, cafes, and bars across the state to remain open until 4 am on July 20 to facilitate live screenings of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.
• This relaxation in Maharashtra was granted under Section 33 of the Maharashtra Police Act, 1951, following a request from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI), Mumbai.
• States/cities including Delhi, Telangana, Bengaluru, and Assam have also extended the operating hours of restaurants, cafés, and bars for the live screening of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.

title: Mizoram declares holiday for schools, half-day for govt offices for FIFA World Cup final
content:
• Mizoram declared July 20 as a full holiday for all schools, colleges, and other educational institutions to allow people to watch the FIFA World Cup final.
• Mizoram government offices will observe a half-holiday on July 20, functioning only from 1 pm to 5 pm.
• Banking institutions in Mizoram are explicitly exempted from the half-holiday on July 20 and will remain operational as usual.

title: FIFA World Cup fuels record trading on Kalshi as platform adds 3 million users
content:
• Kalshi, a prediction market platform, added around 3 million new users during the World Cup, marking one of its biggest growth periods since launch.
• More than $1.2 billion has been wagered through Kalshi's prediction contracts on the FIFA World Cup outcome, making it the company's largest single prediction market to date.
• Kalshi maintains that prediction markets fall under the jurisdiction of the federal Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), which oversees derivatives and swap markets, amidst mounting regulatory scrutiny from several U.S. states.

title: Russia-Ukraine war: 1 killed, 16 wounded as major overnight Moscow's ballistic missile attack hits Kyiv
content:
• Ukraine and the United States reached a political agreement on licences to manufacture Patriot interceptor missiles, with production hoped to begin by the end of the year.
• During Sunday's attacks, Ukraine's military intercepted 18 missiles and shot down 108 of the 125 drones launched.
• Russia launched one of its largest ballistic missile attacks on Ukraine’s capital Kyiv on Sunday, using 41 missiles of different types, killing at least one person and injuring 16 others.

title: Akasa Air prepares for fund raise, revises share capital limit
content:
• Akasa Air revised its authorised share capital from ₹171.50 crore to ₹197.50 crore in a general meeting held earlier this month.
• Last August, Akasa Air had raised approximately Rs 1200 crore by issuing compulsorily convertible preference shares (CCPS).
• Akasa Air plans to raise an additional Rs 1000 crore in equity and debt to meet future capital requirements and expansion.
